result: I would erase the idea that anyone’s worth is conditional—tied to wealth, nationality, race, gender, productivity, or any other invented boundary. The moment that notion dissolved, every system built on ranking human value would collapse: poverty, caste, supremacies, even the quieter cruelty of looking away. In its place, the default setting would become: every person is inherently, irrevocably enough. Everything else—economies, governments, technologies—would have to rebuild on that single, un-negotiable axiom.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
